prompt: Give me a short pep talk before I start a hard task I have been putting off all morning.
response:
You don’t have to feel ready—you just have to begin. The first 2 minutes are the bridge; once you’re on it, momentum will do the rest. You’ve handled tougher things than this, and future-you is begging for that sweet wave of relief when it’s done.

Do this now:
- Stand up, shake it out, one deep breath.
- Open what you need and do the tiniest first move: one messy line, one email draft, one bullet.
- Set a 10-minute timer. When it rings, you can stop—or ride the momentum.

You’ve got this. Start small. Start now.
